title: What Is Credit?
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can credit be used as a financial tool rather than a burden?
What steps can you take to build good credit habits?

options.label: Free money
options.reflection: Credit is not free money - it must be repaid with interest, making it costly if not managed properly.

options.label: Borrowed money that must be repaid
options.reflection: Exactly! Credit is borrowed money that must be repaid, usually with interest charges.

options.label: A gift from the lender
options.reflection: Credit is a loan that must be repaid, not a gift. Lenders expect repayment with interest.

options.label: An investment in your future
options.reflection: While credit can enable investments, it's fundamentally borrowed money that requires repayment.

options.label: Anytime you want to buy something
options.reflection: Using credit for unnecessary purchases can lead to debt problems and financial stress.

options.label: Only during emergencies
options.reflection: While emergencies may require credit, it's most helpful when you plan for repayment in advance.

options.label: For luxury items you can't afford
options.reflection: Using credit for unaffordable luxury items often leads to financial difficulties and debt.

options.label: When repayment is planned
options.reflection: Exactly! Credit is helpful when you have a clear plan for repayment and can afford the payments.

options.label: Your income level only
options.reflection: While income matters, creditworthiness depends on multiple factors including payment history and debt levels.

options.label: Your physical assets only
options.reflection: Assets are one factor, but creditworthiness is primarily determined by how you manage debt and make payments.

options.label: Your payment history and debt management
options.reflection: Exactly! Payment history, debt-to-income ratio, and responsible debt management determine creditworthiness.

options.label: Your age and life stage
options.reflection: Age alone doesn't determine creditworthiness - responsible financial behavior is what matters most.

options.label: Free service charge
options.reflection: Interest is not free - it's the cost of borrowing money that you must pay to the lender.

options.label: The cost of borrowing money
options.reflection: Exactly! Interest is the fee you pay for the privilege of borrowing money over time.

options.label: Bonus money from the lender
options.reflection: Interest is an expense for borrowers, not a bonus. It's the price of using borrowed funds.

options.label: Discount for early repayment
options.reflection: Interest is the ongoing cost of borrowing, separate from any early repayment discounts or fees.

stages.prompt: How does credit affect your financial future?

options.label: It can create opportunities when used wisely
options.reflection: Exactly! Responsible credit use builds credit history and can enable important financial milestones.

options.label: It always creates financial problems
options.reflection: When used responsibly, credit can build financial opportunities and improve your financial standing.

options.label: It has no impact on your future
options.reflection: Credit usage significantly impacts your financial future through credit scores and borrowing capacity.

options.label: It automatically makes you wealthy
options.reflection: Credit is a tool that requires responsible management - it doesn't automatically create wealth.
